You're invited to a SURPRISE birthday party for Marco's 28th!

Date: Saturday, May 16
Time: 6:00 PM
Place: Our apartment — 2385 Union Ave, Apt 14, San Jose

Adri

Language & Communication: Mia's vocabulary is above average for her age group. She's using 2-3 word phrases consistently and loves naming animals during circle time.

Social Development: Mia shares well with her peers and enjoys playing alongside other children. She has started engaging in cooperative play, which is right on track.

Motor Skills: Fine motor is progressing nicely. Mia loves finger painting and is getting better with utensils at snack time. Gross motor is strong. She's a confident climber on the play structure.

Areas to Watch: Mia still has some difficulty settling for nap time. We're working on a calming routine. This is very common at her age.

emails.subject: Your 2-year-old this week: Big emotions, bigger personality

Your toddler at 25 months

What to expect this week:

Language explosion: Your child may be putting 2-3 words together now. Try narrating your day to build vocabulary.

Big feelings: Tantrums are normal at this age. Stay calm and offer simple choices to help them feel in control.

Sleep tip: If bedtime is a battle, try moving it 15 minutes earlier. Overtired toddlers have a harder time falling asleep.

Activity idea: Fill a bin with dried pasta and let your toddler scoop and pour. Great for fine motor skills!
